title: AAR — CapSpace (/fantasy) lag + `database is locked` on sim
scope: Symptoms: 1. The fantasy app (`/fantasy`) "still has a massive lag when I click it" — even after the earlier precompile/Babel fix (`docs/aar-capspace-precompile.md`), which had only addressed the *client* cost. 2. Simming threw a popup: "Simulation finished with 1 game(s) failing. Played 0 successfully. First error: database is locked."

rationale.item: Every fantasy submodule had its own ensure_schema() shaped as a full WAL write transaction (plus a leaked, never-closed connection) called on almost every public function, producing dozens of write+commit round-trips per request — causing both lag (fsync-class WAL commits serialized behind the single writer lock) and 'database is locked' (read→write upgrade refused under contention with a running sim, which busy_timeout does not cover).

Round 2: the real cost is build_slate_data() — which scans the whole season's game_*_stats for the ~175-player pool — rebuilt many times per request; capspace-app.jsx renders an empty div while walletState === undefined.
Round 3: home() ran _settle_all() inline before emitting any HTML, and the client gated the entire app on walletState === undefined.
Round 4: the sim and the fantasy app shared one SQLite file; fantasy writes collided with the sim's WAL writer.

Round 5: settle_bets/settle_entries held an open write transaction and called wallet.credit() (a second connection writing the same DB) inside the loop → self-deadlock, silently swallowed by _settle_all's per-game exception catch.

actions.description: Memoize ensure_schema() per resolved DB path via new @once decorator; applied to all nine ensure_schema() functions; reset() hook for tests
Defensive retry-on-locked: execute()/executemany() run through _retry_on_locked(), a short bounded backoff (6 tries, 50ms→…); non-lock OperationalErrors propagate immediately
Round 2: memoize build_slate_data() per slate_date in data.py, invalidated by the played-game count; returns a shallow copy
Round 2: windowed _build_logs() — ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_id ORDER BY game_date DESC) caps each player to the latest 5 rows in SQL (~14k-row scan to ~875)
Round 2: non-blank loading state — capspace-app.jsx loading branch renders a 'CapSpace · Loading tonight's slate…' splash; bundle rebuilt via tools/build_capspace.sh

Round 3: background, debounced settle — _settle_all() invoked via _kick_settle(), a non-overlapping (Lock), rate-limited (≥6 s apart) daemon thread; home()/_safe_slate, /api/wallet, /api/activity kick it and return immediately
Round 3: fast wallet read; best_streak reads a persisted record (wallet.rec_get) kept current by streak.settle() instead of recomputing the grade walk inline
Round 3: removed the walletState === undefined gate; app renders immediately using balance injected in __CAPSPACE_DATA__; bundle rebuilt
Round 4a: removed every inline settle()/settle_bets() from the read paths; /api/activity reads the bet feed via new read-only sportsbook.activity_bets()

Round 4b: CapSpace gets its own database file <sim-db>-capspace.db via new fdb.py shim (opens fantasy file as main, ATTACHes sim DB as sim); modules import fdb as db; one-time best-effort migration fdb._ensure_migrated lifts pre-split CapSpace tables (column-aware, idempotent; sim-side copies left orphaned)

Round 5: fixed settle_bets and settle_entries to grade all rows, write + commit settlements, then loop wallet.credit() calls once the lock is released (matching streak.settle / buyins.settle_one's 'commit FIRST, then credit')

risk_posture: No engine, schema, or scoring logic changed — only *when* the lazy DDL runs and how transient lock collisions are handled.
Winnings still credit; they just appear on the next poll.
sim-side copies are left orphaned rather than dropped, so the migration never writes to the sim DB

validation_claims.claim: Instrumented run: 45 ensure_schema() calls (5× across all 9 modules) produce exactly 9 executescript runs; a further 30 wallet reads add 0; reset() forces a rebuild.
Retry helper: succeeds after a transient lock; re-raises non-lock errors.
pytest o27/tests → 106 passed. pytest o27v2/tests → 129 passed, 1 skipped.
Round 2: slate cache 20 calls → 1 build; bumping the played count forces a rebuild; returned blobs are mutation-isolated.
Windowed _build_logs returns exactly the latest 5 games per player, most recent first, form sparkline oldest→newest.
Rebuilt bundle: node --check passes; jsdom render shows the splash while the wallet fetch is pending and the full app (sidebar + 15 KB) once it resolves.

Round 3: GET /fantasy/ 14 ms; /api/wallet, /api/activity, /api/slate each ~15 ms (were 15-26 s). Settle runs exactly once across rapid calls, on the capspace-settle daemon thread — never the request thread.
validation_claims.check_method: Time endpoints via Flask test client

validation_claims.claim: jsdom: the full app (sidebar + balance) renders even while the wallet fetch is pending forever; no blank/splash gate remains.
Round 4 migration: an existing cap_wallet/cap_records in the sim DB is copied into the fantasy file; balance/records read correctly; the sim-side copy is left untouched.

Isolation: a wallet.credit() completes in ~10 ms while another connection holds the sim DB's write lock (BEGIN IMMEDIATE) — no database is locked. Writes land in the fantasy file; the sim DB is unchanged.
Web smoke: /fantasy/ and all /fantasy/api/* return 200 in <70 ms on the split DB.
Round 5: a winning bet settles, credits exactly once (+payout), raises nothing, and is idempotent on a second pass.

non_actions.item: Verification against the live deployed DB in a browser (round 1)
non_actions.reason: the page should be loaded once post-deploy to confirm the felt loads quickly and a concurrent sim no longer reports database is locked

non_actions.item: Date floor on build_slate_data's recent-form log scan
non_actions.reason: the windowed query trims row transfer but not the scan. A date floor (only games within N days of the slate) would cut it further.

non_actions.item: Closing the connection opened in ensure_schema()
non_actions.reason: now bounded to once per module, but a `with get_conn()` would be tidier

non_actions.item: Shared/post-sim trigger for background settle across multiple workers
non_actions.reason: Background settle is per-process; with multiple workers a few passes can overlap across processes (idempotent, lock-tolerant via the db retry), but a shared/post-sim trigger would be cleaner.

residual_risks: Background settle is per-process; with multiple workers a few passes can overlap across processes.
A single build_slate_data still scans the season for the pool's recent-form logs.
Endpoint timings to be re-measured on the live deploy (round 2).
open_hooks: reset() hook in _schema_once for test suites that drop the CapSpace tables
Date floor for slate log scan
section_headers_original: AAR — CapSpace (/fantasy) lag + `database is locked` on sim
Diagnosis — one root cause behind both
Round 2 — the page was *blank*, not just slow
Round 3 — the page was unreachable: settle was on the critical path
Round-3 fix — settle is never on the request path
Round 4 — the real conflict: sims vs. the fantasy app on one DB
4a — fantasy is read-only on the request path
4b — CapSpace gets its own database file
Round 5 — a settle self-deadlock the split DB exposed
unmapped_content:
Round-2 net effect claim: "the per-request build_slate_data work drops from N rebuilds of a full-season scan to a single windowed build, so /api/{wallet,slate,activity} should return in ~1 s instead of 15-26 s, and the page paints a splash immediately. (Endpoint timings to be re-measured on the live deploy.)"
Round-2 diagnosis detail: HTML, JS bundles (byte-identical to committed artifacts), MIME types, and the injected __CAPSPACE_DATA__ blob were all correct; live endpoint timings /api/wallet 14.9 s, /api/slate 18 s, /api/activity 26 s; all three funnel through _settle_all(). Round-3 diagnosis: the HTML document itself took 15.7 s to return, then /api/wallet another ~12 s. Round-4b detail: fantasy and sim table names are disjoint, so SQLite resolves each unqualified name to the right attached file, and reading an attached WAL DB never blocks the sim. Round-5 detail: because _settle_all catches per-game exceptions, this was silent: sportsbook/DFS winnings simply never credited.
parser_notes: No date anywhere in document; marked undated. Multi-round AAR (5 rounds) collapsed into one record per instructions.
